adds.rationale: First publicly traded pure-play quantum-computing company; profile explicitly names quantum as a next-wave priority and prior to IONQ's Oct 1, 2021 listing there was no liquid US-listed pure-play. Trapped-ion approach is differentiated from superconducting peers and accessible via AWS Braket and Azure Quantum.

news_evidence.summary: IonQ closes business combination with dMY Technology Group III and begins trading on NYSE as IONQ; first publicly traded pure-play quantum-computing company.

adds.rationale: Sole North American manufacturer of large naval nuclear reactors plus growing Canadian SMR supply-chain footprint via partnerships with NuScale and GE Hitachi. Hits both nuclear (next-wave) and defense exposure; complements URA/CCJ (uranium fuel cycle) with downstream reactor manufacturing.

news_evidence.summary: BWXT awarded ~$2.2B in US Naval Nuclear Propulsion Program contracts (including future-year options) for manufacture of naval nuclear reactor components and fuel.
NuScale advances SMR supply-chain work with BWXT Canada, expanding BWXT's role in the small modular reactor build-out.

adds.rationale: Data-center CPU and accelerator share gainer; pending Xilinx acquisition (announced Oct 2020, regulatory approvals progressing through 2021) brings FPGA and AI-inference IP that broadens AMD's AI silicon stack. Diversifies AI-compute exposure beyond NVDA/TSM concentration.

news_evidence.summary: AMD reports Q3 2021 revenue of $4.31B, +54% YoY, driven by EPYC data-center server CPU share gains; reiterates Xilinx deal on track to close.

rationale_overall: Three adds advance the profile's next-wave thesis on distinct fronts: IONQ opens the first quantum pure-play slot, BWXT layers nuclear-reactor manufacturing on top of the URA/CCJ fuel-cycle exposure (and adds defense alongside LMT), and AMD broadens AI-silicon exposure beyond the NVDA/TSM concentration. No removes: at end of 2021 the AI majors and existing thematics are still in buildup/surge phase per the 'trim before crest' rule, and 10 free slots make defensive trimming unnecessary. All adds carry strictly pre-2022 dated evidence; engineered-biology, solar, and wind excluded.
notes: Cap=24 with 10 slots free at as-of; using 3 of 3 add allowance, 0 removes. Wave buckets used: quantum (IONQ), nuclear (BWXT), AI (AMD) - all from valid spec list. IONQ confirmed pre-date listing (2021-10-01). No post-date events cited (Ukraine invasion, Fed hike cycle, ChatGPT, GPT-4, DeepSeek, etc. all suppressed). Self-critique pass: every evidence date is <= 2021-12-31 and the AMD bullet deliberately stops at Q3 2021 earnings rather than the Feb 2022 Xilinx deal close.
